articles.title: Severe hot winds likely in 95 mandals over two days in Andhra - The New Indian Express
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
VIJAYAWADA: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority has warned of severe heatwave conditions across the State over the next two days.
Managing Director Prakhar Jain said that on Thursday (April 23), 95 mandals are likely to face severe hot winds, while another 101 mandals may experience heatwave impact. On Friday (April 24), forecasts indicate 50 mandals under severe heatwave and 52 mandals under hot wind conditions.
On Wednesday, Kadapa recorded the highest temperature at 44.4°C, while 202 mandals reported maximum temperatures above 41°C. District-wise severe heatwave alerts for April 23 include Srikakulam (19), Vizianagaram (24), Manyam (3), Alluri (5), Polavaram (8), Visakhapatnam (1), Anakapalle (13), Kakinada (10), East Godavari (6), Eluru (3), and NTR (3).

articles.title: AP Disaster Management warns of severe heatwaves in 95 mandals - The Siasat Daily
articles.full_text:
Amaravati: As many as 95 mandals across Andhra Pradesh will witness several heatwaves on Thursday, April 23, the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) said.
Severe heatwaves are likely to impact 95 mandals, including Srikakulam (19), Vizianagaram (24), Manyam (3), Alluri (5), Polavaram (8), Visakhapatnam (1), Anakapalli (13), Kakinada (10), East Godavari (6), Eluru (3), and NTR (3), while heatwave conditions are expected in another 101 mandals.
“Severe heatwave and heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in several mandals across the state today,” said APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain in an official release.
He said that people have been advised to take necessary precautions against the heat’s intensity.
Meanwhile, he noted that on Wednesday, April 22, a maximum temperature of 44.4 degrees Celsius was recorded in Kadapa, while temperatures above 41 degrees Celsius were recorded in 202 mandals.
On Friday, severe heatwaves are likely in 50 mandals and heatwaves in 52 mandals, he said.

APSDMA Warns of Severe Heatwaves in 90 Mandals Across AP
Severe heatwave and heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in several mandals across the state today: APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain
Amaravati: As many as 95 mandals across Andhra Pradesh will witness several heatwaves on Thursday, the APSDMA said .Severe heatwaves are likely to impact 95 mandals, including Srikakulam (19), Vizianagaram (24), Manyam (3), Alluri (5), Polavaram (8), Visakhapatnam (1), Anakapalli (13), Kakinada (10), East Godavari (6), Eluru (3) and NTR (3), while heatwave conditions are expected in another 101 mandals.

articles.title: AP Disaster Management Warns of Extreme Heat and Weather in Andhra Pradesh - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
AP Disaster Management Warns of Extreme Heat and Weather in Andhra Pradesh
Prakhar Jain, Managing Director of the Andhra Pradesh Disaster Management Authority, has warned that the state is likely to experience diverse weather conditions in the coming days. On Wednesday, Kadapa recorded a maximum temperature of 44.4°C, with over 200 mandals experiencing temperatures above 41°C.
He forecasted that on Thursday, severe heatwaves are expected to affect 95 mandals across the state, while another 101 mandals may face regular heatwaves. On Friday, these figures are expected to decrease, with 50 mandals experiencing severe heatwaves and 52 facing regular heatwaves. Jain also mentioned the possibility of sporadic moderate rains accompanied by lightning in several districts, along with gusty winds reaching speeds of 40–60 km/h.
Severe heatwaves are predicted to impact 95 mandals in districts including Srikakulam (19), Vizianagaram (24), Manyam (3), Alluri (5), Polavaram (8), Visakhapatnam (1), Anakapalli (13), Kakinada (10), East Godavari (6), Eluru (3), and NTR (3).
